results.title: Add and manage attachments on records and activities
excerpts:
customers use HubSpot for their business.](https://blog.hubspot.com/customers)
[Product Updates Blog Updates on the latest releases from HubSpot’s Product team.](https://www.hubspot.com/product-updates)
Knowledge Base
CRM
Records
Add and manage attachments on records and activities
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Available with any of the following [subscriptions](https://knowledge.hubspot.com/billing/w...
Content:
[All products and plans](https://www.hubspot.com/products/crm)
Additional subscriptions required for certain features
Upload and attach files to records and activities to keep context and resources accessible in HubSpot. Attachments help teams reference documents, images, or other files directly from records such as contacts, deals, and services. You can also manage existing attachments by renaming, filtering, or removing them as needed. ## Understand requirements Subscription required A *Professional* or *Enterprise* subscription is required to use team specific permissions . The permissions required to view attachments depend on how the attachments were added: * When attachments are added on a record , users must have Notes view permissions to view them. Notes view permissions options:
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Add or remove an attachment on a record To add an attachment on a record: 1. Navigate to yo...
Content:
Click the **name** of the record. In the right sidebar, scroll down to the *Attachments* card. Click **Add** , then select:
**Your computer** : upload a new file from your computer. **Existing file in HubSpot** : select an existing file in your HubSpot account. In the right panel, click the **name** of the file you want to attach. To remove an attachment on a record: 1. Navigate to your records (e.g., contacts, projects):
**Contacts:** In your HubSpot account, navigate to **CRM** > **Contacts** . **Projects:** In your HubSpot account, navigate to **CRM** > **Projects** . Click the **name** of the record. In the right sidebar, scroll down to the *Attachments* card. Click the **X** in the upper right of the attachment. In the dialog box select from the following:
**Delete** : permanently delete the attachment on the record and the logged activity.
Section Title: ... > Add or remove an attachment on a record To add an attachment on a record: 1. Navigate to yo...
Content:
This option appears for sent one-to-one emails or emails logged via the HubSpot sales extension . **Detach** : remove the attachment added to the record but keep it in the files tool . This option appears for attachments added via the files tool .
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Add an attachment to an activity To add create an activity and add an attachment to it: 1. ...
Content:
Click the **name** of the record. In the left sidebar, click the **icon** for the activity. In the activity editor, click the attach **attach icon** , then select **Upload new** to add a file from your computer, or **Choose existing** to select a file already added to HubSpot. Select the **file** , finish adding the activity's details, then click **Log [name of activity]** or **Create note** . To add an attachment to an existing activity:
Navigate to your records (e.g., contacts, projects):
Contacts: In your HubSpot account, navigate to **CRM** > **Contacts** . Projects: In your HubSpot account, navigate to **CRM** > **Projects** . Click the **name** of the record. Click the **Activities** tab. Hover over an activity and click the **text box** .
Section Title: ... > Add an attachment to an activity To add create an activity and add an attachment to it: 1. ...
Content:
In the text editor, click the attach **attach icon** , then select **Upload new** to add a file from your computer, or **Choose existing** to select a file already added to HubSpot. Select the **file** , then click **Save** . **Please note:** when you [send one-to-one emails](https://knowledge.hubspot.com/one-to-one-email/send-and-reply-to-one-to-one-emails) in HubSpot or log an email using HubSpot Sales , non-inline attached files are automatically added to the *Attachments* section of the record. Any inline attachments will not be added. ## Manage attachments Once you've added attachments, you can view all attached files on each record, filter them to locate the attachments you need, and rename or remove them. 1. Navigate to your records (e.g., contacts, projects):
**Contacts:** In your HubSpot account, navigate to **CRM** > **Contacts** .
Section Title: ... > Add an attachment to an activity To add create an activity and add an attachment to it: 1. ...
Content:
**Projects** : In your HubSpot account, navigate to **CRM** > **Projects** . Click the **name** of the record. In the right panel, scroll down to the *Attachments* section. If you have more than 5 attachments, click **Show more** to display 5 additional files. To view and manage the full list of attachments, **View all attachments** . In the right panel:
Enter **text** in the *Search name and file type* text field, to search for a file by name or type. Click **File type** , then select the **checkboxes** of types of files you want to view. Click **Source** , then select the **checkboxes** of sources you want to filter by. For example, select *Calls* to view attachments added via a call activity or *Manual upload* to view files added from the *Attachments* section. Click a **column header** to change the sort order of the attachments.
Section Title: ... > Add an attachment to an activity To add create an activity and add an attachment to it: 1. ...
Content:
For example, click the *Uploaded date* column to sort by the oldest or newest attachment. Click the **name** of the attachment to preview it. In the preview, you can do the following:
**Download** . The file will be downloaded to your computer. **Share internal link** : share a link to the file preview in HubSpot. The link will be copied to your clipboard and can be shared with other users in your account. **Share 24-hour link** : share a link that expires after 24 hours. In the address bar, copy the **link** . This link to the file can be shared externally and will be accessible for up to 24 hours. Hover over a **file** , click **Actions** , then select an option:
**Rename** : in the dialog box, enter a new name, then click **Rename** to confirm. **Download** : download the file to your computer. **View** : preview the file in a new tab.
Section Title: ... > Add an attachment to an activity To add create an activity and add an attachment to it: 1. ...
Content:
**Remove** : in the dialog box, confirm that you'd like to delete or remove the attachment. For attachments uploaded on the record or on an activity, click **Delete** to permanently delete the file. For attachments added to the record from the files tool , click Detach . The file will be removed from the record, but can still be accessed in the files tool. Select the **checkboxes** next to the attachments, then select an option:
**Download** : download multiple files to your computer. **Remove** : remove multiple files. In the dialog box, confirm that you'd like to delete or remove the attachments and click **Remove all** . Files will be either deleted or removed based on how they were added. Learn more about deleting and detaching .
Section Title: ... > Add an attachment to an activity To add create an activity and add an attachment to it: 1. ...

content_type *string*
The content type of the file
Section Title: Intercom API (2.9) > Conversation attachment files
Content:
Example: "application/json"
data *string*
The base64 encoded file data.
Example: "ewogICJ0ZXN0IjogMQp9"
name *string*
The name of the file.
Example: "test.json"
```
{
  "content_type" : "application/json" ,
  "data" : "ewogICJ0ZXN0IjogMQp9" ,
  "name" : "test.json" 
}
```

results.title: Working with Email Attachments for Your Dynamics CRM Integration
excerpts:
Section Title: Working with Email Attachments for Your Dynamics CRM Integration
Content:
Before we begin designing the data flow, it would be prudent to check our Dynamics 365 interface to make sure that we have a proper file size setting when working with email attachment records. Essentially, we want to make sure that both the file sizes of our attachment files fall within the **maximum file size value** , and that the file types of your attachments are not blocked by your application. To check, we will first sign into Dynamics 365, select **Advanced Settings** , and then select Settings, Administration, and System Settings. The size limit setting may be adjusted under the **Email** tab and by default, this will be set to 5120 kilobytes.
Once all of the above is checked and confirmed, we will move into our SSIS design.
 ... 
Section Title: Working with Email Attachments for Your Dynamics CRM Integration > Design and Configuration
Content:
Note that the KingswaySoft CRM Destination Component accepts image datatypes/Binary columns to be mapped directly to the "body" field in the CRM **activitymimeattachment** entity. At a glance, this may seem like a mismatch between the datatypes (NTEXT and IMAGE), but behind the scenes, the file content is saved in the Dynamics database in base64 format. Our software would automatically encode the file content before sending the request to the server. If preferred, you can always encode the file content yourself using the EncodeBase64() function in our **Premium Derived Column** component which offers nearly 300 functions. With that being said, in the upcoming section, you can see that we map the FileContent column directly to the body field without any transformation.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Writing using the CRM Destination component
Content:
**ObjectId** : The unique identifier (GUID) of the parent object with which the attachment will be associated. Specifically, this would be the GUID of the email activity.
**ObjectTypeCode** : The type code of the entity with which the attachment will be associated. In our example, we will be using the string representation 'email,' and please note that this is case-sensitive.
**Body** : The file content we have in the Source.
**MimeType** : The media type of our attachment. In our example, the attachment is a png file, so the mimetype we will use is ‘image/png’.
**FileName** : name of the file, including the extension. An example would be “attachment1.png”.
Section Title: ... > Writing using the CRM Destination component
Content:
Please note that we have left the **ActivityId** column **unmapped** , which is the unique identifier ( **GUID** ) of the activity with which the attachment will be associated. This is an important rule when working with the **activitymimeattachment** entity. You should only map **activityid** field, or otherwise the combination of objectid and objecttypecode fields. You should not map both. When doing so, you will run into errors.
After all is done, you may now execute the package or data flow which should write email attachment record(s) successfully and the file attachments should be properly associated with the related email activity records in the system.

results.title: Configure file attachment capability for a chat widget | Microsoft Learn
excerpts:
Section Title: Configure file attachment capability > Customer experience of attaching a file
Content:
When the file attachment capability is enabled for customers, the attachment icon displays in the chat widget. A customer can then use the **Attach** icon to send files. The drag-and-drop operation isn't supported.
When the conversation is going on, the file attachment is scanned and then uploaded. After the conversation ends, the attachment will be stored in the Annotations collection in Dataverse, which is an Azure BLOB-based entity. Customers can access attachments in transcripts by selecting the attachments, which are retrieved from the Dataverse entity.
